traceability.content: AEGIS-TOP-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §2 (SoC Top)
Domain isolation at top level; independent clock/reset per domain
DFT scan interface fuse-gated in production
Scan pins must be tied off in production silicon
RT domain uses independent clock and reset for isolation
Application and Security domains not instantiated in Phase 2
Scan enable must be tied to 0 in production
AEGIS-RT-TOP-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §2 (RT Domain)
All safety mechanisms integrated at this level
CSR outputs wired to all submodules for deterministic config
Both peers OK (normal operation)
CSR-controlled scrub enable
CSR-controlled interval
CSR-controlled sleep request
CSR-controlled wake request
Deny-by-default; CSR-configured regions
Machine mode always
AEGIS-RT-TCLS-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Lockstep)
2oo3 voting ensures correct output even with single core fault;
Output is majority of 3 inputs; handles single core fault
Any pairwise mismatch is flagged as a single-cycle pulse
Counter resets on match; accumulates on mismatch
AEGIS-RT-TCLS-002 — Mismatch counter (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Increment counter on mismatch (saturate at threshold)
Reset on match (prevents transient noise accumulation)
Quarantine triggered when mismatch count reaches threshold
AEGIS-RT-TCLS-003 — Quarantine timing (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Quarantine acknowledged — promote spare core
Threshold reached — initiate quarantine
AEGIS-RT-XDR-002 — ARCHITECTURE.md §7 (Xdrone Extension)
All Xdrone ops have fixed latency (no data-dependent timing)
Fixed latency per opcode — no data-dependent timing
dt=1.0 (Q8.8) — fixed for RT determinism
Fixed latency per opcode
Wait for sub-module valid or cycle count
Generic ops use cycle counter
Default prevents latch inference
AEGIS-RT-XDR-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §7 (Xdrone Extension)
Fixed-latency dispatch for all Xdrone operations
Xdrone uses custom-0 (0x0B) and custom-1 (0x2B) opcode space
R-type format for Xdrone instructions
AEGIS-XDRONE-QMUL-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §4 (Xdrone)
Fixed-point arithmetic with saturating overflow
All intermediate products computed in 32-bit to prevent overflow
Products computed from input ports, not registered values
Q8.8 products are Q16.16; right-shift by 8 to rescale to Q8.8
AEGIS-RT-DEC-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (Decoder)
Illegal instruction → trap to handler (no undefined behavior)
All opcodes have explicit decode (no implicit defaults)
Illegal instruction trap
All immediates sign-extended to 32 bits
Compressed instructions expanded to 32-bit equivalents
M ops share opcode 0x33 with R-type; funct7[5:4]=01
Explicit case for every opcode; default = illegal
Compressed instructions should be expanded in fetch
Reserved funct3
FLW — load float to FPU register
All other opcodes are illegal
AEGIS-RT-EXC-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Exception Handling)
All exceptions trap to machine mode — no delegation
MRET restores previous privilege and interrupt state
RISC-V privileged spec compliant
Fixed vector address
Priority: MRET > ECALL > EBREAK > Illegal
MRET returns from handler, restores mepc
Shadow bank swap on ECALL/EBREAK (context switch)
AEGIS-RT-ATOM-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (A Extension)
Reservation set invalidated on any write to reserved address
SC.W fail returns 1 (non-zero) per RISC-V spec
Single-entry reservation set (sufficient for RT single-core)
Sets reservation on current address
Reservation matches — SC succeeds
No reservation or address mismatch — SC fails
Invalidate reservation on external write to same address
AEGIS-RT-TCLS-004 — Mismatch counter (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Counter resets on match; saturates at threshold
Increment on mismatch, saturate at threshold
Reset on match (no false accumulation)
AEGIS-RT-ALU-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (ALU)
FPU ops delegated to rt_fpu with FTZ mode
FPU ops delegated to rt_fpu — ALU returns 0 if reached
Default prevents latch
AEGIS-RT-WDG-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (Watchdog)
Watchdog timeout → SMU fault → safe-state (irreversible)
Counter cannot be disabled — only kicked (restarted)
Counts down from i_timeout; kick resets to i_timeout
Load timeout on enable transition
Kick restarts the counter
Counter expired — timeout!
Timeout is latching — requires reset to clear
AEGIS-RT-CSR-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §9 (CSR Map)
Machine-mode only access prevents unprivileged CSR modification
Only Machine mode (priv=2'b11) can access CSRs
AEGIS-RT-CSR-002 — Privilege gating (ISO 26262-6:2018 §6.4.3)
Reserved reads as 0
Writes only allowed in Machine mode
AEGIS-RT-PIPE-001 — Pipeline interface (ARCHITECTURE.md §3)
Pipeline always advances unless stalled
AEGIS-RT-MULDIV-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (M Extension)
Divide-by-zero returns all-ones; signed overflow returns max
No data-dependent timing — dummy cycles pad fast results
All multiply variants computed; select based on funct3
Divide-by-zero → all-ones; signed overflow → max positive
Divide by zero → -1
Signed overflow → max positive
Dummy cycles pad fast results for constant timing
Output result at fixed latency
AEGIS-RT-BRANCH-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Determinism)
Fixed branch latency prevents timing side-channels
No data-dependent timing (constant evaluation time)
IRQ redirect has absolute priority
AEGIS-RT-C-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (Compressed ISA)
Unrecognized compressed instructions → illegal 32-bit instruction
Expansion is deterministic — no data-dependent timing
Default = illegal instruction (0x00000000 = ADDI x0, x0, 0)
AEGIS-RT-PIPE-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (Pipeline)
No data hazard may produce incorrect result — stall or forward
All stalls are deterministic — no data-dependent stall duration
Detect when decode reads a register that execute or writeback
Must stall 1 cycle — load data not available until WB
MULDIV/FPU/Xdrone occupy EX for multiple cycles
Flush on branch mispredict (not implemented in Phase 2 —
Forward from EX or WB to avoid unnecessary stalls
AEGIS-RT-REG-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Context Switch)
Shadow banks enable ≤18-cycle hardware context swap
Register x0 always reads as 0 (RISC-V spec)
Shadow bank preserves previous context for fast swap
x0 is hardwired to 0; writes to x0 are ignored
Swap active and shadow banks
x0 hardwired to 0 — writes ignored
x0 always returns 0
AEGIS-RT-CORE-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (RT Core)
Deterministic timing; no cache miss paths; fixed interrupt latency
4-stage pipeline — IF, ID, EX, WB + Xdrone multi-cycle
If fetch returns compressed, expand; otherwise pass through
No data hazard may produce incorrect result
Forward from EX or WB to resolve RAW hazards
EX forwarding uses wb_result (which captures prior EX output)
Use forwarded data when not stalled
FTZ prevents subnormal latency variance
FTZ always enabled for RT determinism
Timeout → SMU fault → safe-state
Always enabled in RT mode
All exceptions trap to machine mode
4-stage in-order + Xdrone multi-cycle
SMU safe-state or debug halt freezes pipeline
Pipeline stall — hold current state
Context switch on ECALL/EBREAK → shadow bank swap
Shadow bank swap on IRQ entry
Shadow bank swap + PC redirect to handler
WDG timeout
AEGIS-RT-INT-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Determinism)
Fixed 12-cycle entry; no cache miss paths; priority encoder hardwired
Hardwired priority — highest pending + enabled IRQ wins
Priority encoder output is one-hot; convert to index for vector table
Fixed 12-cycle entry regardless of IRQ source
AEGIS-RT-INT-002 — Entry latency (ISO 26262-6:2018 Table D.3)
Default prevents latch inference
AEGIS-RT-DFT-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §6 (DFT)
Scan enable must be hardwired to 0 in production (fuse-gated)
Scan chains must not compromise TCLS lockstep integrity
In functional mode (scan_enable=0), these are don't-care
Shift scan data when in test mode
Scan enable must be tied to 0 in production silicon
AEGIS-XDRONE-KALMAN-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §4 (Xdrone)
Fixed-point with saturating arithmetic
No floating-point — deterministic fixed-point only
All operations use deterministic fixed-point
AEGIS-SEC-CT-001 — CERTIFICATION.md §3.2 (Side-Channel Resistance)
Dummy operation insertion ensures constant power profile
Latch result from real operation
AEGIS-SEC-PMP-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §4 (Security)
Deny-by-default for safety peripherals; 16 configurable regions
Deny-by-default — unconfigured regions deny all access
Reset all regions to inactive (deny-by-default)
Deny-by-default; first matching region determines outcome
Deny by default
Write to read-only region = violation
AEGIS-SEC-SMU-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §4 (Safety Monitor)
Fault aggregation is combinational (1-cycle); safe-state trigger
Fault latch persists until explicitly acknowledged
AEGIS-SEC-SMU-002 — Fault latch persistence (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
FSM ensures safe-state is requested within 2 cycles of threshold
AEGIS-SEC-SMU-003 — Severity classification per ISO 26262-5:2018
Latches highest-priority fault until acknowledged
Clear latch on acknowledge (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Latch first fault (prevents overwriting with lower-priority)
Update if new fault has higher severity
Bit-per-fault-source history for diagnostic analysis
AEGIS-SEC-SMU-004 — Fault history for ISO 26262-5 diagnostic
Counters track fault accumulation per severity level
Triggers safe-state when aggregation threshold is breached
AEGIS-SEC-SMU-005 — Safe-state trigger timing (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Check for threshold breach or software request
Assert safe-state request for power orchestrator
Hold safe-state request until acknowledged
Default prevents latch inference (ISO 26262-8:2018 §8.4.3)
AEGIS-SEC-BOOT-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §4 (Secure Boot)
Boot stub is read-only (OTP ROM); immutable after manufacture
Stub — always pass for Phase 1
AEGIS-SEC-CRYPTO-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §4 (Crypto)
All crypto ops go through constant_time_wrapper
AEGIS-MEM-SP-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Scratchpad)
All writes go through ECC encoder; all reads through decoder
All reads are ECC-checked; corrected data available immediately
AEGIS-MEM-SCR-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (ECC Scrubber)
Scrubber corrects latent single-bit errors before they accumulate
IDLE→READ→CHECK→CORRECT→IDLE cycle
Read issued; data available next cycle (async read)
If single-bit error, correct and write back
Write corrected data back to same address
Default prevents latch inference
AEGIS-MEM-MUX-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §9 (Memory Map)
CSR/Xdrone spaces are privilege-gated
Memory map per ARCHITECTURE.md §9.2
Default 0 for unmapped regions
Always ready (no backpressure)
AEGIS-MEM-ECC-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (ECC Protection)
Single-bit errors corrected in 1 cycle; double-bit errors
SECDED guarantees correction of single-bit errors and
Syndrome identifies exact error position for single-bit errors
Non-zero syndrome indicates error; syndrome value = error position
SECDED classification logic:
AEGIS-MEM-ECC-002 — Error classification per ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3
XOR the error bit position to flip the corrupted bit
No data correction for check-bit errors
AEGIS-MEM-SP-002 — ARCHITECTURE.md §5 (Scratchpad Controller)
Dual-bank allows simultaneous access + background scrubbing;
Address MSB selects bank; each bank = 256 KB
Dual-bank allows core access + scrubber on different banks
Always ready (no backpressure in TCM)
Any single/double error from either bank triggers SMU fault
Scrubs one address per SCRUB_INTERVAL_CYCLES
Advance scrub address; wrap around
AEGIS-PWR-ISO-001 — Power domain isolation (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Isolation prevents undefined signals from powered-down domain
When iso_en=1, output clamps to CLAMP_VALUE regardless of input
When iso_en=0, signal passes through transparently
AEGIS-PWR-PDIF-001 — Power domain interface (ISO 26262-5:2018)
All signals active-high for consistent control semantics
AEGIS-PWR-WAKE-001 — Wake sequencing (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Power domain must stabilize before logic is enabled;
Start stabilization counter on wake request
Stabilization period complete
AEGIS-PWR-ORCH-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §6 (Power Management)
Safe-state transition is irreversible until external reset;
States are one-hot encoded for glitch-free transitions
Safe-state is highest priority; once entered, only reset exits
AEGIS-PWR-ORCH-002 — State transition timing (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
SMU safe-state request has absolute priority
Check SMU request even during sleep prep
SMU request can wake from sleep to safe-state
Only external reset can exit safe-state
AEGIS-PWR-ORCH-003 — Irreversible safe-state (DO-254 §5.3.1)
Remain in safe-state until external reset
Default prevents latch inference (ISO 26262-8:2018 §8.4.3)
Signals are derived combinationally from state for immediate effect
Isolation clamps outputs to safe defaults (0)
Power domain always-on during SLEEP (retention requires power)
AEGIS-PWR-RET-001 — Retention register (ISO 26262-5:2018 §8.4.3)
Retention register preserves critical state during power gating;
Shadow register retains value even when main power is gated
Restore from shadow register on wake
Shadow captures main_reg only when retention_en rises (edge-triggered save)
Capture on rising edge of retention enable
Detect mismatch between main and shadow after restore
AEGIS-SEC-IOP-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §8 (IOPMP)
Deny-by-default for DMA to safety peripherals
AEGIS-INT-AXI-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §8 (Interconnect)
RT traffic has absolute priority over all other masters
RT always granted in Phase 1 (single master)
AEGIS-INT-AXI-002 — ARCHITECTURE.md §8 (Timeout)
Prevents deadlock from unresponsive slaves
Timeout — trigger SMU fault
AEGIS-INT-TT-001 — ARCHITECTURE.md §8 (Interconnect)
Fixed schedule prevents starvation and non-deterministic latency
Fixed priority: master 0 (RT) > master 1 (Security) >
